text.title: Ruby on Rails Service Objects Examples
text.summary: Worked example: extract PlaceOrderService from OrdersController#create.

example.context: Rails 7 app; OrdersController#create is 95 lines: validates, creates order, decrements inventory, charges card, sends email, returns response. Lives in one method; tested only via request spec.
example.walk: Step 1: action = PlaceOrder, context = Order. Step 2: app/services/orders/place_order_service.rb; constructor takes user:, gateway:, inventory:, mailer:; default to real implementations. Step 3: #call(params) wraps Order.create + InventoryItem.decrement + PaymentGateway.charge in transaction; returns ServiceResult.success(order:) on green; on InsufficientStock returns ServiceResult.failure(error: :insufficient_stock). Step 4: controller create action 6 lines — result = service.call(order_params); render result.data or error. Step 5: spec/services/orders/place_order_service_spec.rb — 12 cases (happy path, insufficient stock, gateway down, invalid params) — all isolated mocks.
example.output: Controller #create 6 lines; service 80 lines; tests run in 200ms (vs 4s for request specs).
